Alright, JB here. Time for your daily motivation slash skills training. I want to tell you a great story. One of the things that I always do in a live event, this is classic, right? I learned this from Dr. Bandler. First, I still haven't done it and I kind of put my own twist on it, right? He says to everybody in the room, everyone look around the room and I want you to find the color red in the room, right? He goes, look for red, look for red. It's like, I want to see like nine years ago, it's ten years, more, eleven years ago, right? Look for red, look for red, look for red, right? When everyone's looking around, looking for red, looking for red, he goes, close your eyes. He goes, alright, now, why do you think about everything that you saw that's brown and everyone starts laughing? You didn't see anything. You can't remember anything that was brown. Why? Because you were only looking for it. When you look for something that's all you see, now there was brown all over the room, but no one could think of anything that's brown. We delete out what we're not looking for. So when someone says, look for red, look for red, look for red, your skin, you look for everyone's red, you say, close your eyes, okay, tell me everything that's purple, you're like, oh, oh, oh, oh, let me look for, you don't see it. Now, the leap that you can make here, why does that matter? Well, it's pretty profound when it comes to how you go about achieving success. It's what I talk about when it's about vision focus, bifurcated focus. You know, you can't, if you're so tunnel visioned on your outcome, on your vision, right, tunnel vision, right, and you're not scanning the horizon, you're just looking, all you're looking for is what your vision tells you to be looking for. Opportunity, you're not going to see them, they'll fly over your head. That's one side of the equation, but it gets worse. Because the second part of that equation is now where he'd say, look for red, look for red, okay, who could tell me something in the room that's red? And someone would invariably, oh, that person's shirt is red, okay, great. What else is red? The flag is red, great. What else is red? Oh, the cop is red, okay, great. What else is red? The mock is red, okay, great. What else is red? The lamp shade is red, okay, great. What else is red? The trimming on the shades is red, okay, great. What else is red? The exit side is red. What else is red? And on and on they go, 10, 15, 20 things. And then the red starts to thin out, right? And invariably, because I've been did this myself, I've probably done this 50 times, right, I said, I keep going and going, and then invariably about maybe 10, 15, 10 in, right? So what? That person's boots are red. You look at the boots and they're green. I'm like, what do you mean those boots are green? Oh, yeah, I guess they are. Your brain starts making shit up to be right. You actually start inventing things. So you know, there's a seek and you shall find. Well, the way it really is seek and you shall invent. If you can't once you run out of things you're looking for, your brain will actually trick you and make things seem a different way to be in line with what you're expecting. That's what beliefs do. When you have, if you believe, let me just tell you my own story here, okay? If you, when I was first getting started on the speaking circuit, this is a true story, right? You know, I had a lot of bad press from back in the day. This is before I already, not that 12 years later, different story. Redeem myself has been a movie. I've had huge success stories, right? I'm talking about the very, very beginning and I had this belief like, you know, because one journalist wrote, oh, he's a crook and he's already exist. He's that, right? And I was like, oh, meanwhile, I had a lot of positive experiences along the way. I've been hired by companies. Virgin had already sponsored me. Symantec, another billion dollar company, some radio stations, Delta Airlines, big companies, right? Broker troops had sent their big, the biggest firms and banks and HSBC had hired me. Right? But one company had said, oh, we can hire you because you're too controversial with your past. And I had this belief my past will always haunt me. My past will always haunt me. And because of that, I didn't aggressively seek out corporate business for many years because of that belief, because I really believed in my heart that that was true. One comment was all it took. Now, ultimately, he was Leo DeCaprio, actually we were preparing for him. He's like, I told him this. He's like, you know, what are you crazy? And he wrote me a letter and a shot of video and I overcame that belief. But the point was, is that I had all this evidence that it wasn't true. I've been hired by all these major companies. But because of one situation that hit me at the wrong time made me feel bad inside, I developed a limiting belief about my own marketability. And I really didn't aggressively market myself to these big companies. I stayed with small companies. And then I broke out of that eventually. And I said, what am I doing? I now work for all the biggest companies in the world. But that belief held me back. I want to ask you a question. Now, honestly, what limiting beliefs do you have that are really holding you back? If you don't think you have any, guess what? You're fouling your first limiting belief. You don't have any. We all have them.
